issue_body:
The `should_ignore_error` method on the Flask application class should be deprecated and eventually removed.

This method was originally added as part of the code to keep the request context around for use in the debugger, tests, etc. However, that is not how context preservation works anymore.

The method's intention was to allow ignoring certain errors during debugging so that cleanup would still run immediately. It also causes the exception to not be passed to teardown handlers, but there doesn't seem to be any reason to hide that, and handlers can already choose what to do if they're passed an error.

The method is only documented in the API reference, not in any other pages. There is no test for it. It results in an extra function call on every single request only to always return `False`. This can be deprecated then removed.
